markers.source_table: Reynolds et al. (2021) Fig. S5, general literature

other_findings.finding: Reynolds et al. (2021) annotated skin MPs by aligning to blood MPs using TransferAnchors; CD8A, CD163, CD14 and CCR7 were chosen for CyTOF protein validation
Two macrophage states (Mac1, Mac2) are distinct from monocytes; monocyte-derived macrophages (Mono mac) also identified
other_findings.source_table: Reynolds et al. (2021) main text and figure legend

other_findings.finding: Human blood monocytes classified into: classical (CD14++ CD16-), intermediate (CD14++ CD16+), and non-classical (CD14+ CD16++)
Classical monocytes are rapidly recruited to tissues in inflammatory responses and can differentiate into macrophages or DCs

evidence_quotes.quote: We observed 14 states of mononuclear phagocytes (MPs) in human skin (Figs. 3A-B and S5A) that we annotated by aligning skin and blood MPs using TransferAnchors function in Seurat (Fig. S5B) and expression of MP marker genes
evidence_quotes.source_file: Reynolds et al. (2021) PDF full text
